summary: Added a repo-root `build.sh` helper for common decomp build workflows: clean builds, selective object deletion by pattern, optional map generation, non-matching builds, EPILOGUE_PROCESS toggling, console clearing, and syncing successful builds into an ignored `expected/build` tree. The Makefile was adjusted so `EPILOGUE_PROCESS` can be overridden from the command line via `?=`, and `.gitignore` now excludes the generated `expected/` directory.

key_files.role: New shell helper wrapping `make` with quick flags for clean/rebuild/map/non-matching/epilogue/expected-build workflows.
Changed `EPILOGUE_PROCESS` from an unconditional assignment to an overridable default, enabling command-line control from `build.sh`.
Ignored the generated `expected/` directory used by the new sync option.

what_changed: Added `-c` to run `make clean` before building.
Added `-r pattern` to delete matching object files under `./build` using a `*/${pattern}*.o` pattern, with clean taking priority.
Added `-e` to sync `build/` to `expected/build/` after a successful make run using `rsync -a --delete`.
Added `-m`, `-n`, and `-f` flags to pass `GENERATE_MAP=1`, `NON_MATCHING=1`, and `EPILOGUE_PROCESS=1` to make.
Added `-x` to clear the console before running the script and `-h` help output.
Changed the Makefile's `EPILOGUE_PROCESS` assignment to `EPILOGUE_PROCESS ?= 1`, making it externally overrideable.
Added `expected/` to `.gitignore` so synced expected build output is not tracked.
smart_moves: Centralized common local build variants in a small shell script instead of requiring contributors to repeatedly remember Makefile variable names.
Made `EPILOGUE_PROCESS` overrideable with `?=`, which is necessary for a wrapper script to pass `EPILOGUE_PROCESS=0` or `1` explicitly.
Protected the `expected` sync path by only syncing after a successful make result; failed builds print an error and do not overwrite the expected tree.
Used selective object deletion as a faster alternative to full clean builds when only a subset of files needs rebuilding.
Ignored `expected/` because it is generated local comparison/build output rather than source.
decomp_lessons: For decomp projects with frequent rebuild/test cycles, wrapper scripts can reduce friction by exposing common Makefile variables as short flags.
Makefile variables intended for tooling control should use conditional assignment (`?=`) or otherwise allow command-line overrides.
Local expected-output directories used for comparison should be kept out of version control.
A pattern-based object rebuild option can speed matching workflows by avoiding full `make clean` when only specific translation units need recompilation.
Build helpers should avoid updating comparison baselines after failed builds.
naming_conventions: `GENERATE_MAP`, `NON_MATCHING`, and `EPILOGUE_PROCESS` are uppercase Makefile feature toggles passed as `0`/`1` values.
The script uses lowercase shell variables such as `clean`, `rebuild_pattern`, `expected`, `generate_map`, `non_matching`, `frank`, and `clear`.
The expected-build mirror is named `expected/build/`, and the ignored top-level generated directory is `expected/`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `NON_MATCHING=1` through `build.sh -n` when intentionally compiling non-matching code paths.
Use `GENERATE_MAP=1` through `build.sh -m` when a linker map is useful for symbol/layout inspection.
Use `EPILOGUE_PROCESS=1` through `build.sh -f` to enable the epilogue-processing build path when needed.
Use `build.sh -r pattern` to delete matching `*.o` files under `./build` and force targeted recompilation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify or replace `build.sh`.
Search for later changes to `EPILOGUE_PROCESS` defaults or epilogue processing workflow.
Search for uses of `expected/build` or scripts that compare `build/` against `expected/`.
Find build tooling PRs that add more Makefile variables or wrapper flags.
